page: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with 24 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processing power is driven by an Intel Broadwell architecture CPU running at 2.0 GHz with 24 physical cores and single-thread-per-core execution. This instance features shared CPU allocation and includes a GPU array consisting of four NVIDIA A16 GPUs from the Ampere family, delivering a total of 64 GB of VRAM.

Benchmark results place this server in the average tier for general CPU and memory operations, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 41473.88 and a PassMark CPU score of 35340.23. However, the GPU-accelerated architecture excels in specific workloads. In LLM inference tests using a medium 7B model, prompt processing speeds reach a top-tier 614.8 tokens per second, while text generation achieves a strong 32.83 tokens per second. For smaller 135M models, prompt processing scales to 8464.24 tokens per second. Memory bandwidth is measured at 137.58 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 56.27 GB/sec for large block write operations.

The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, exclusive CPU performance, though the presence of 24 physical cores mitigates resource contention. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server provides a dense allocation of GPU resources and memory relative to its CPU tier, making it highly suitable for multi-stream video decoding, virtual desktop infrastructure, and moderate machine learning inference. The 1200 GB NVMe SSD local storage and 256.0 GB RAM also support data-intensive applications, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a Cloud GPU server designed for memory-intensive and GPU-accelerated workloads. It features 24 Intel Broadwell vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The hardware configuration is highlighted by four NVIDIA A16 Ampere GPUs providing a total of 64 GB of VRAM. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing, reaching 614.8 tokens per second on a 7B model, alongside strong text generation capabilities. While the shared CPU allocation is a consideration for sustained CPU-bound tasks, the high memory density and quad-GPU setup offer excellent qualitative value for parallel processing. This server is highly suited for machine learning inference, video transcoding, and database operations.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 24 Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, 1200 GB NVMe storage, and four NVIDIA A16 GPUs with 64 GB total VRAM, optimized for inference and parallel workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram features 24 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and four NVIDIA A16 GPUs for high-density parallel processing.
tagline: A quad-GPU accelerated server combining high memory capacity and NVMe storage for parallel processing and machine learning inference workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA A16 Ampere GPUs with 64 GB total VRAM
24 Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory
1200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier LLM prompt processing performance on 7B models
Optimized for machine learning inference and video transcoding
